headline: Round 6 reworks the tray's front-of-bin treatment per modeler-notes-v6. The boss + scoop indent grab feature from round 5 is fully removed. Front wall now has VARIABLE HEIGHT along X (corner sections at z=18, lowered center 50mm wide at z=10). Side-wall to front-wall corner fillets (r=12) eliminate the round-5 'fangs.' Interior floor ramp re-oriented from convex (hump) to concave (parabolic slide-curve), terminating at the lowered-center top z=10. Cradle is unchanged from round 5.

dimensions.notes: Round-6: NO CHANGES vs round 5. Cradle dimensions, geometry, and watertight status all unchanged. Render time ~80s at draft quality ($fn=100, top_fillet_steps=24).

Round-6 substantive rebuild on tray-only. Mesh y-extent reverts 96.7 → 94.2 (round-5 grab boss removed entirely; tray y now equals ext_d=int_d+2*wall_t exactly). Volume drops 76.14 → 38.49 cm³ (49% reduction): the round-5 ramp solid mass is replaced by a thinner concave parabolic surface, the front wall is shorter on average (variable z=10..18 vs uniform z=18), and the boss is gone. Top z=29.933 (0.07 mm under nominal — final fillet roll's last inset). Render time ~16s at draft quality (vs ~80s at ship).

totals.change_breakdown: Cradle: ~unchanged (136.67 → 136.61 cm³, -0.06 cm³ — round-off only). Tray: -37.65 cm³ (boss removed, ramp solid lighter, front wall shorter on average). Net 17.7% reduction.

fix1_tray_variable_height_front_wall.description: Round-5's uniform-height (z=18) front wall + boss + scoop indent is replaced with a variable-height profile: corner sections at full z=18, lowered center 50mm wide at z=10. Concave quarter-arc r=8 transitions between corner and center. The lowered center IS the grab feature — a finger reaches over the 10mm-tall opening and hooks the front lip from above. Construction: a 2D X-Z polygon describing the AIR-ABOVE-WALL profile (with side fillets, corner flats, transition arcs, lowered center) is extruded along Y over the front-wall slab and subtracted from the full-height tray box.
spec_diffs: DELETE: tray_front_wall_h, tray_front_wall_top_treatment_note, grab_boss_proud, grab_boss_width, grab_boss_height_z, grab_boss_corner_r, grab_boss_construction, grab_scoop_arc_r, grab_scoop_chord_z, grab_scoop_depth, grab_scoop_center_z, grab_scoop_width, grab_scoop_x_center, grab_scoop_construction, tray_mesh_y_with_grab_boss, ramp_arc_radius, ramp_arc_construction, front_wall_top_z_above_desk

ADD: tray_front_wall_corner_h=18, tray_front_wall_center_h=10, tray_front_wall_center_w=50, tray_front_wall_transition_arc_r=8, tray_front_wall_corner_fillet_r=12, tray_front_wall_construction (description)
UPDATE: echoedDimensions.tray.y 96.7 → 94.2 (boss removed, mesh y reverts to ext_d)
UPDATE: tray_exterior_d 94.2 (unchanged) — round 5 had documented mesh-y separately; now mesh-y = ext_d exactly
UPDATE: label_drop_note + label_clears_front_wall_by_min_mm to reflect dual front-wall heights (corner z=29 desk-ref clears tape exit by 35-50mm; lowered center z=21 desk-ref clears by 43-58mm)
ADD: tray render quality knobs (tray_fn_draft, tray_fn_ship, tray_top_fillet_steps_draft/ship, tray_ramp_arc_steps_draft/ship)

deviations.what: Front wall top edge has no separate r=2 top-edge roll fillet
deviations.why: The variable-height profile already encodes concave arcs (transitions r=8, side fillets r=12) along the entire top edge — the wall top is curved-by-construction. Adding a separate r=2 roll on top would either A) require a much more complex slab-stack across the variable profile, or B) sit awkwardly on top of the existing arcs. Decision: skip the separate r=2 top-edge roll on the front wall; the variable-height arcs ARE the front-wall top treatment. Back/side walls keep the r=2 rolled cap unchanged from round 5.

deviations.what: Side-wall to front-wall fillet exists only WITHIN the front-wall slab (y in [ext_d-wall_t, ext_d])
deviations.why: The fillet sweep (z=30 → z=18, r=12) is encoded into the front-wall-top cutter polygon, which extrudes only across the front-wall slab thickness. At y < ext_d-wall_t (in the side-wall extent BEHIND the front-wall slab), the side wall stays full-height z=30. There is a Y-direction step at y=ext_d-wall_t where the side wall's top suddenly drops from z=30 to z=18 over zero Y distance. Visually this is mostly hidden because the side wall top is itself filleted by the back/sides r=2 top-edge stack. A double-curvature fix (X-fillet + Y-fillet at the upper outer corners) was deemed beyond round-6 scope; if round-7 critique flags the Y-step, that's the next iteration. Documented in tray.scad source comments.

fix2_side_wall_to_front_wall_corner_fill.description: Concave quarter-arc r=12 at the upper outer corners of the front wall, sweeping the wall's top edge from the side wall's z=30 down to the front-wall corner's z=18. Replaces the round-5 hard step that produced the visible 'fangs.' Encoded into the front-wall-top cutter polygon as `left_side_fillet` and `right_side_fillet` arcs.

fix3_concave_floor_ramp.description: Round-5's circular arc ramp (convex pan-edge, hump up into cavity) is replaced with a quadratic curve z(y) = 1.6 + 8.4 * ((y - 62.6) / 30)^2 from (62.6, 1.6) to (92.6, 10). Tangent to the flat floor at the back (slope = 0 at y=62.6). Concave from cavity side (curve dips below the chord); from a finger sliding forward, slope is gentle near the back and steepens monotonically toward the front (like climbing a slide). The new ramp_front_z=10 (down from 18) terminates the ramp at the lowered-center top, producing a unified geometry where the ramp's front edge IS the lowered center's top.
fix3_concave_floor_ramp.spec_diff: UPDATE: ramp_front_z 18 → 10. UPDATE: ramp_arc_radius DELETED (no longer a circular arc). ADD: ramp_orientation='concave_from_cavity_side', ramp_construction (description of quadratic curve).

deviations.what: Ramp is a quadratic curve, not a circular arc
deviations.why: Modeler-notes-v6 fix3 specified a concave-from-cavity-side circular arc with center above chord, suggested r=25-35. Round-6 modeling derived that a circular arc passing through both endpoints (62.6, 1.6) and (92.6, 10) with center above chord ALWAYS dips below floor (z=floor_t=1.6) at its lowest point — proven by the quadratic in r that has negative discriminant. The chord rise (8.4mm over 30mm) is too small relative to the chord length to keep a concave-from-cavity arc above the floor. The notes anticipated this: 'Alternative if direct concave arc placement is geometrically tricky: use a Bezier curve or compound construction.' A quadratic z(y) = 1.6 + 8.4 * ((y-62.6)/30)^2 satisfies all three properties simultaneously (monotonic rise, concave from cavity, always above floor), tangent to flat floor at the back. Documented in spec.json under ramp_construction. Self-supporting in face-up print orientation (every layer of the ramp surface sits on solid floor material below).

fix4_draft_render_quality_adoption.description: Both SCAD files declare quality knobs at the top: $fn=100, top_fillet_steps=24, ramp_arc_steps=32 (tray only). Iteration renders at draft quality. Shipper bumps to ship quality at delivery via -D overrides.
fix4_draft_render_quality_adoption.spec_diff: Render quality settings already present in tray.scad and cradle.scad from commit b2695ec; spec.json adds documentation of draft vs ship quality settings.

render_time_comparison.comparison_note: Tray renders 5x faster than cradle (16s vs 80s). Cradle's CGAL face count is 6490 (printer pocket + tray slot + concave fillet between sections + smooth top fillet stack), tray's is 2252 (smaller part with simpler concave operations). Both are at draft quality. Round-5 ship-quality render times were ~15min cradle + ~1min tray; draft is ~10x faster on cradle, ~4x faster on tray.

specDeviations.reason: Round-5 grab boss (extending 2.5mm proud of front wall +Y face) is removed entirely. Mesh y-extent now equals ext_d = int_d + 2*wall_t = 91 + 3.2 = 94.2 mm exactly.
Fix 1: front wall has variable height. Lowered center is the grab feature.
Fix 3: ramp now terminates at the lowered-center top (z=10), not the corner top (z=18). Unified geometry between ramp front edge and lowered center top.

specDeviations.round_5_value: circular arc r=22, convex from cavity (hump up)
specDeviations.reason: Fix 3 functional reorientation; circular arc with these endpoints + concave + above-floor has no real solution, so quadratic is used.
Boss removed; envelope and mesh agree.
Concave parabolic ramp is fully self-supporting in face-up print orientation (always angles up-and-forward, never overhangs).

features.name: base_plate
features.description: Stepped base plate with r=10 hero corner radius. Footprint follows stepped_footprint_raw — narrow under printer section (X=12..98, Y=0..160), wider under shelf (X=0..110, Y=160..254.9). Concave r=10 quarter-arc on both ±X sides at the printer→shelf transition. UNCHANGED from round 5.

features.name: low_wall_block
features.description: 25 mm tall stepped perimeter wall block. UNCHANGED from round 5.

features.name: printer_shelf_concave_fillet
features.description: r=10 concave quarter-arc on both ±X sides where the 86 mm printer section transitions to the 110 mm shelf section. UNCHANGED from round 5.

features.name: host_object_proxy
features.description: RENDER-ONLY printer proxy box (78 × 152 × 143 mm). UNCHANGED from round 5.

featuresTray.name: tray_floor
featuresTray.description: Solid floor at z = 0..floor_t (0..1.6 mm) at the back portion of the bin (y = wall_t..ramp_back_y = 1.6..62.6). Full interior width (x = wall_t..ext_w-wall_t = 1.6..101.6).

featuresTray.name: tray_walls_back_sides
featuresTray.description: Back wall (-Y) and ±X side walls — vertical, rounded_rect outer outline with r=3 vertical edge fillet, uniform 1.6 mm thickness. Top edges (z = ext_h = 30) carry a continuous r=2 quarter-arc fillet rolled inward via 24-step slab stack at $fn=100 draft (64 ship). UNCHANGED from round 5.

featuresTray.name: tray_front_wall_corners
featuresTray.description: Variable-height front wall — outer corner sections at full z = 18. X ranges: x in [0, 18.6] (left) and x in [84.6, 103.2] (right). Material at y in [ext_d-wall_t, ext_d] = [92.6, 94.2]. Each corner section's top profile sweeps via a side-fillet (r=12, x in [wall_t, wall_t+12] sweeping z=30→18) into a flat segment at z=18 (5mm wide), then a transition arc (r=8) down to the lowered center.

featuresTray.name: tray_front_wall_lowered_center
featuresTray.description: Lowered center of the front wall, 50mm wide (x=26.6..76.6), top at z=10. THE GRAB FEATURE: a finger reaches over the 10mm tall opening and hooks the front lip from above. Replaces round-5's boss + scoop indent.

featuresTray.name: tray_front_wall_transitions
featuresTray.description: Concave quarter-arc r=8 transitions between the corner-section flat (z=18) and the lowered center (z=10). Two arcs, mirrored. Each arc spans 8mm in X and 8mm in Z. Concave-from-above (curve dips below chord midpoint).

featuresTray.name: side_wall_to_front_wall_fillets
featuresTray.description: Concave quarter-arc r=12 at the upper outer corners of the front wall, sweeping z=30 down to z=18 across an 12mm X span (wall_t..wall_t+12 on the left, ext_w-wall_t-12..ext_w-wall_t on the right). Replaces the round-5 hard step that produced 'fangs' at these corners. Encoded into the front-wall-top cutter polygon. NOTE: the fillet operates only within the front-wall slab (y in [ext_d-wall_t, ext_d]); a Y-direction step at y=ext_d-wall_t is documented as a known simplification.

featuresTray.name: interior_floor_ramp
featuresTray.description: Concave parabolic floor surface near the front of the bin. Curve z(y) = 1.6 + 8.4 * ((y-62.6)/30)^2 connects (y=62.6, z=1.6) (back end, tangent to flat floor) to (y=92.6, z=10) (front end, at lowered-center top). Concave from cavity side — curve dips below the chord. From a finger sliding forward: slope gentle at back, steepens monotonically toward front (like a slide). Self-supporting in face-up print orientation. Full interior X width (x = wall_t..ext_w-wall_t).

cradle.bed_face: base plate bottom (z = 0 — flush with bed, NO feet)

tray.bed_face: floor (z = 0 on bed) — face up (open top up)
tray.notes: Round 6 confirms face-up. Variable-height front wall is a 'missing material' feature, not an overhang — fully self-supporting. The transition arcs in X-Z are also self-supporting. The concave parabolic ramp surface curves DOWN from the floor surface (every layer of the ramp surface sits on solid floor material below) — no overhangs at any layer. The side-wall to front-wall fillet sweeps from a higher z to a lower z in the X direction — no overhangs at any layer. Verify in slicer; no supports expected.

userOrientationConfirmed.user_front: +Y face (tray slides in/out here; printer faces user; lowered center grab on tray)

render_method: Direct openscad (2021.01) invocation with explicit eye+center cameras (--camera=eye_x,eye_y,eye_z,center_x,center_y,center_z). cli-anything-openscad presets remain unusable per all prior rounds' lessons. Combined-cradle-tray harness files (.combined-bare.scad, .combined-use.scad) used for views that show both parts in the same frame. Tray-only views use tray.scad directly; cradle-only views use cradle.scad directly. Render quality DRAFT throughout ($fn=100, top_fillet_steps=24, ramp_arc_steps=32).

tray_lowered_center_cutout.details: tray-user-front shows the lowered-center cutout clearly: corner sections at z=18 with smooth concave fillets sweeping up to the side walls, transitioning via concave arc r=8 down to a 50mm flat segment at z=10. The opening reads as a clear 'reach in here' affordance — a finger fits comfortably above the 10mm tall lowered section. Much more obvious than round-5's 2.86mm-deep capsule indent.

tray_no_more_fangs.details: tray-user-front and tray-user-front-threequarter both show smooth concave sweeps at the side-wall to front-wall transitions. No hard z-steps, no pointed protrusions. The r=12 fillet matches the 12mm height drop from z=30 to z=18, producing a continuous quarter-arc.

tray_concave_ramp_orientation.details: tray-user-front-threequarter shows the interior ramp as a smooth concave surface dipping below the chord between back and front endpoints. From a finger's-eye view sliding forward across the floor, the surface gradually rises, slope steepening monotonically toward the front — no hump, no inflection. Profile verified: z(y) = 1.6 + 8.4 * ((y-62.6)/30)^2. Tangent to flat floor at back (slope=0), reaches z=10 at front with slope 0.56 (~29° from horizontal — printable, no overhang).

tray_unified_ramp_to_lowered_center_geom.details: Ramp terminates at z=10 = lowered-center top z. The ramp's front edge IS the back edge of the lowered-center cutout — single continuous z=10 line across the front of the bin. No discontinuity, no awkward join.

no_round5_grab_boss.details: Boss + scoop + capsule end-caps fully removed. Mesh y-extent reverts 96.7 → 94.2 (= ext_d exactly).

cradle_unchanged.details: cradle.scad untouched. STL and renders match round-5 geometry. Only difference: render time (80s draft vs 15min ship in round 5). All round-3, 4, 5 cradle improvements preserved.

uncertainItemsFromV6Notes.lowered_center_50mm_wide_10mm_tall: Reads cleanly in tray-user-front. The width-to-height ratio (50:10) feels balanced — the cutout dominates the lower-front but doesn't overwhelm the bin's closed-kanban character. If round-7 critique flags it as too cramped (finger access still tight), bump width to 60-70 or drop center height to 8.
uncertainItemsFromV6Notes.front_wall_transition_arc_r_8: Renders smoothly at draft quality (24-step slab approximations of the arc). Could be bumped to r=10 for hero-radius consistency; r=8 was chosen to match the 8mm height difference (corner z=18 to center z=10) for a clean quarter-arc.
uncertainItemsFromV6Notes.side_wall_to_front_wall_fillet_r_12: Reads as a smooth sweep, eliminating the round-5 fang. The 12mm fillet matches the 12mm height drop (z=30 to z=18) for a clean quarter-arc. Doesn't eat too much side-wall material — the fillet is confined to the front-wall slab thickness in Y.
uncertainItemsFromV6Notes.concave_ramp_curve_depth: Quadratic curve with z'(62.6)=0 and z'(92.6)=0.56 produces a shape that's visually subtle from most angles (the curve is gentle), pronounced from the threequarter angle. Mid-point of curve at y=77.6 has z=3.7 (chord at 5.8 — 2.1mm dip below chord). If round-7 critique flags as too subtle, switch to higher exponent (e.g. cubic) for sharper steepening at front.
uncertainItemsFromV6Notes.ramp_terminating_at_z_10: Ramp now ends at the lowered-center top (z=10), shorter rise than round-5's 16.4mm. The visual effect is gentler — the bin reads as predominantly flat with a soft front lift, not a dramatic ramp. Consistent with the v3 minimalism direction.
uncertainItemsFromV6Notes.draft_quality_render_times: Cradle ~80s, tray ~16s. 5x speedup on cradle vs round-5 ship quality (15min). Iteration cycle is now fast enough to do multiple round-trips without time pressure.

renderTimeRecord.comparison_to_round_5_ship_quality: Round 5 cradle STL took ~15 minutes at $fn=200 + 64-step fillet stack. Round 6 cradle STL takes ~80 seconds at $fn=100 + 24-step. ~10x speedup. Tray went from ~60 seconds (round 5 ship) to ~16 seconds (round 6 draft) — 4x speedup. Combined: a full 7-render iteration round drops from ~30+ minutes to ~8 minutes.
